prompt: This code fails to compile with 'local variables referenced from a lambda must be final or effectively final'. What is the bug?
choices: Predicate<Integer> cannot be used with stream().filter()
Lambdas can only capture static fields, not local variables
threshold is reassigned after declaration, so it is not effectively final and cannot be captured by the lambda
System.out::println is not a valid method reference for forEach
explanation: A lambda can capture a local variable only if it is final or effectively final (never reassigned). 'threshold = 10' on the third line makes threshold mutable, breaking effective finality. Fix: either remove the reassignment (keep threshold=5) or use a separate effectively-final variable: int limit = 10; Predicate<Integer> above = n -> n > limit;
